service: Ensure the safe and lawful use of buildings and properties by enforcing the Building Code and the Zoning Resolution.
oneLiner: This enforcement exists because deadly building disasters and unsafe, overcrowded tenements in the 1800s and early 1900s showed that without a dedicated code-enforcement agency, builders and landlords would cut corners at the cost of lives.
summary: New York's modern building-safety bureaucracy grew out of tragedy: a "Superintendent of Buildings" post was created in 1860 after the Elm Street fire on the Lower East Side killed 20 people, and the 1901 Tenement House Act created a citywide Buildings Bureau after investigators documented dangerous, overcrowded housing conditions. A unified, citywide Department of Buildings was established in 1936. Today DOB enforces the Building Code and Zoning Resolution across more than a million buildings, issuing permits, licensing trades, and responding to structural emergencies.

sources.label: New York City Department of Buildings - Wikipedia
